title: Fix: cx3.7 closed with only migration SQL — RealtimeService Dart code committed in cx3.11 beads close (from flutter_cleanapp-cx3.7)
description: Review of flutter_cleanapp-cx3.7 found: The close commit (0a28080) only contains supabase/migrations/003_enable_realtime.sql. The actual RealtimeService Dart implementation (lib/core/realtime_service.dart, screen subscriptions in home_screen.dart, activities_screen.dart, comments_screen.dart) was bundled into the beads close commit for cx3.11 (addf7e3 'beads: close flutter_cleanapp-cx3.11'). Similarly, cx3.10 and cx3.11 had their implementation code bundled into beads metadata commits rather than separate implementation commits. The code IS present and working, but the commit history is misleading — beads close commits should only contain .beads/ changes. This is a workflow/process issue for the Lead to address in future decomposition.
close_reason: Process issue noted — commit hygiene for parallel workers. No code fix needed.

title: Epic: CleanApp Frontend — Cleaning Schedule Management UI
description:
## Goal
Build the complete frontend for a bathroom cleaning schedule management app for an apartment building. The app allows residents to see when it is their turn to clean, track cleaning tasks via a checklist, and view the upcoming schedule on a calendar.

## Success Criteria
- App has 3 screens accessible via a BottomNavigationBar: Home, Activities, Calendar
- Blue-themed color palette with light/dark mode toggle
- All screens use mock data (no backend yet — Supabase integration is future work)
- App passes flutter analyze, dart format, and flutter test

## Key Constraints
- Frontend only — no Supabase integration yet
- Mock data provider supplies all data (users, schedules, tasks)
- Must follow project AGENTS.md code style (package imports, const constructors, super.key, etc.)
- Material Design 3 with blue color scheme
- Spanish-language UI strings (the app is for Spanish-speaking users)
close_reason: Closed

title: Define app theme system with light/dark mode and blue color palette
description:
## Files
- lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart (create)

## What to do
Create a theme configuration file that provides both light and dark ThemeData objects using Material Design 3.

### Class: `AppTheme`
Abstract final class (no instantiation) with these static members:

```dart
static const Color seedColor = Color(0xFF1565C0); // Blue 800
static final ThemeData lightTheme = ThemeData(...)
static final ThemeData darkTheme = ThemeData(...)
```

Both themes MUST use:
- `useMaterial3: true`
- `ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: seedColor, brightness: ...)` for the respective brightness
- `fontFamily: "Roboto"` (default, just be explicit)
- BottomNavigationBar theme: selected item uses `colorScheme.primary`, unselected uses `colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant`
- Card theme: elevation 1, rounded corners (12 radius)
- AppBar theme: `centerTitle: true`, `scrolledUnderElevation: 2`

### Enum: `AppThemeMode`
```dart
enum AppThemeMode { light, dark, system }
```

## Do not
- Import any external packages (only flutter/material.dart)
- Create a ChangeNotifier or state management here — that goes in the shell task
- Use hardcoded colors beyond the seed color — let ColorScheme.fromSeed generate the palette
- Use deprecated ThemeData fields

title: Create mock data provider with sample users, schedules, and tasks
description:
## Files
- lib/data/mock_data.dart (create)

## What to do
Create a class that provides hardcoded mock data for the entire app. This replaces Supabase until backend integration.

### Class: `MockData`
Abstract final class with static getters/methods:

```dart
abstract final class MockData {
  /// The currently "logged in" user (always the first user for now)
  static UserModel get currentUser => users.first;

  /// All residents in the building
  static List<UserModel> get users => [ ... ];

  /// Cleaning schedule entries — one per user, rotating weekly
  /// Generate entries for the past 2 weeks and next 6 weeks (8 total entries)
  static List<CleaningSchedule> get schedules => [ ... ];

  /// The 8 cleaning tasks for a given schedule
  static List<CleaningTask> tasksForSchedule(String scheduleId) => [ ... ];

  /// Default task titles in Spanish
  static const List<String> defaultTaskTitles = [
    "Lavar el retrete",
    "Lavar el lavamanos",
    "Lavar la regadera",
    "Barrer",
    "Trapear",
    "Secar el piso",
    "Sacar la basura",
    "Limpiar las jergas",
  ];
}
```

### Mock users (4 residents):
- id: "u1", name: "Carlos García", apartment: "Depto 1A"
- id: "u2", name: "María López", apartment: "Depto 2B"
- id: "u3", name: "Juan Martínez", apartment: "Depto 3A"
- id: "u4", name: "Ana Rodríguez", apartment: "Depto 4B"

### Schedule generation logic:
- Start from the Monday of 2 weeks ago
- Each user gets assigned one week in rotation (u1, u2, u3, u4, u1, u2, u3, u4)
- Past schedules are marked isCompleted: true
- The current week and future weeks are isCompleted: false
- Use DateTime.now() to determine "current week"

### Task generation:
- tasksForSchedule returns 8 CleaningTask objects, one per defaultTaskTitles entry
- Task IDs: "{scheduleId}_t{index}" (e.g. "s3_t0", "s3_t1", ...)
- All tasks start with isCompleted: false

## Do not
- Use any external packages
- Make network calls or async operations
- Add randomness — data must be deterministic for a given week
- Import flutter/material.dart (only import the model files)
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/data/mock_data.dart exists
2. MockData.users returns exactly 4 UserModel objects with the specified names and apartments
3. MockData.currentUser returns the first user (Carlos García)
4. MockData.schedules returns exactly 8 CleaningSchedule entries spanning 8 weeks
5. Past schedules have isCompleted == true, current and future have isCompleted == false
6. MockData.tasksForSchedule("s0") returns exactly 8 CleaningTask objects
7. Task titles match the 8 Spanish cleaning tasks listed in defaultTaskTitles
8. MockData.defaultTaskTitles has exactly 8 entries
9. flutter analyze passes with zero issues

title: Build Home/Dashboard screen with cleaning status notification
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create the Home screen that shows the current user their cleaning status.

### Class: `HomeScreen` (StatelessWidget)
```dart
class HomeScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  final VoidCallback onNavigateToActivities;
  const HomeScreen({super.key, required this.onNavigateToActivities});
}
```

The screen reads mock data to determine the current user status and renders one of two states:

### State A — User IS responsible for cleaning this week:
Layout (top to bottom, centered, with padding 24):
1. Large icon: `Icons.warning_amber_rounded` (size 80, color `colorScheme.error`)
2. SizedBox(height: 16)
3. Text "¡Te toca hacer el aseo!" (headlineMedium, bold, centered)
4. SizedBox(height: 8)
5. Text "Semana del {startDate} al {endDate}" (bodyLarge, centered) — dates formatted as "dd/MM/yyyy"
6. SizedBox(height: 24)
7. Card containing:
   - ListTile with leading Icon(Icons.person), title: user name, subtitle: user apartment
8. SizedBox(height: 24)
9. FilledButton.icon(
     icon: Icons.checklist,
     label: "Ir a Actividades",
     onPressed: onNavigateToActivities,
   ) — this button calls the callback to switch to the Activities tab

### State B — User is NOT responsible this week:
Layout (top to bottom, centered, with padding 24):
1. Large icon: `Icons.check_circle_outline` (size 80, color `colorScheme.primary`)
2. SizedBox(height: 16)
3. Text "¡Estás libre esta semana!" (headlineMedium, bold, centered)
4. SizedBox(height: 8)
5. Text "Tu próximo turno es la semana del {nextDate}" (bodyLarge, centered) — date formatted as "dd/MM/yyyy"
6. SizedBox(height: 24)
7. Card containing:
   - ListTile with leading Icon(Icons.person), title: user name, subtitle: user apartment

### Logic to determine state:
- Get current user from MockData.currentUser
- Find the schedule entry for the current week (where schedule.date falls in this Monday-Sunday range)
- If that schedule userId == currentUser.id AND isCompleted == false → State A
- Otherwise → State B (find next future schedule where userId == currentUser.id for the "next date")

Wrap the body in a SingleChildScrollView for safety.

## Do not
- Use any state management — this is a StatelessWidget reading MockData directly
- Add any async operations
- Handle the case where no schedule exists (mock data always has schedules)
- Import or reference Supabase
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/screens/home_screen.dart exists with HomeScreen StatelessWidget
2. HomeScreen constructor takes required VoidCallback onNavigateToActivities
3. When current user has cleaning duty: shows warning icon, "¡Te toca hacer el aseo!" text, date range, user card, and "Ir a Actividades" button
4. When current user is free: shows check icon, "¡Estás libre esta semana!" text, next turn date, and user card
5. "Ir a Actividades" button calls onNavigateToActivities callback
6. Dates are formatted as dd/MM/yyyy
7. All text is in Spanish
8. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
9. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed

title: Build Activities screen with cleaning task checklist and finalize button
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/activities_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create the Activities screen showing a to-do checklist of cleaning tasks with a Finalize button.

### Class: `ActivitiesScreen` (StatefulWidget)
```dart
class ActivitiesScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const ActivitiesScreen({super.key});
  @override
  State<ActivitiesScreen> createState() => _ActivitiesScreenState();
}
```

### State management:
- On initState, load tasks from MockData for the current user current-week schedule
- Store tasks in a `List<CleaningTask>` state variable
- If the current user has no schedule this week, show an empty state

### Layout:
The screen has two sections inside a Column:

**Section 1 — Header (inside Padding 16):**
- Text "Actividades de Aseo" (titleLarge, bold)
- SizedBox(height: 4)
- Text "Marca cada tarea al completarla" (bodyMedium, secondary color)

**Section 2 — Task list (Expanded, inside ListView.builder):**
Each task is a CheckboxListTile:
```dart
CheckboxListTile(
  value: task.isCompleted,
  onChanged: (value) => _toggleTask(index),
  title: Text(task.title, style: TextStyle(
    decoration: task.isCompleted ? TextDecoration.lineThrough : null,
  )),
  secondary: Icon(
    task.isCompleted ? Icons.check_circle : Icons.circle_outlined,
    color: task.isCompleted ? colorScheme.primary : colorScheme.outline,
  ),
  controlAffinity: ListTileControlAffinity.leading,
)
```

**Section 3 — Finalize button (inside Padding 16, at bottom):**
```dart
SafeArea(
  child: Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
    child: SizedBox(
      width: double.infinity,
      height: 48,
      child: FilledButton.icon(
        onPressed: _allCompleted ? _finalize : _showIncompleteWarning,
        icon: Icon(Icons.done_all),
        label: Text("Finalizar Aseo"),
      ),
    ),
  ),
)
```

### Behavior:
- `_toggleTask(int index)`: calls setState, replaces task at index with `task.copyWith(isCompleted: !task.isCompleted)`
- `_allCompleted`: getter that returns true if every task in the list has isCompleted == true
- `_showIncompleteWarning()`: shows a SnackBar with text "Aún tienes tareas pendientes por completar" and a warning icon
- `_finalize()`: shows a SnackBar with text "¡Aseo completado! Gracias por tu colaboración" with a success color (colorScheme.primary), and optionally marks all tasks visually as done

### Empty state (no schedule this week):
Show centered Column with:
- Icon(Icons.event_available, size: 64, color: colorScheme.outline)
- Text "No tienes aseo asignado esta semana" (bodyLarge)

## Do not
- Persist state to any backend
- Use any external state management packages
- Navigate away from this screen on finalize
- Add animations or transitions
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/screens/activities_screen.dart exists with ActivitiesScreen StatefulWidget
2. Shows 8 CheckboxListTile items matching the 8 cleaning tasks in Spanish
3. Tapping a checkbox toggles the task completed state and shows strikethrough text
4. Completed tasks show a check_circle icon in primary color
5. "Finalizar Aseo" button exists at the bottom, full width
6. Pressing Finalizar when NOT all tasks are checked shows SnackBar "Aún tienes tareas pendientes por completar"
7. Pressing Finalizar when ALL tasks are checked shows success SnackBar "¡Aseo completado! Gracias por tu colaboración"
8. When user has no schedule this week, shows empty state with "No tienes aseo asignado esta semana"
9. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
10. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed
close_reason: 7561298 Add Activities screen with cleaning task checklist and finalize button

title: Build Calendar screen showing upcoming cleaning schedule
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create the Calendar screen that shows the upcoming cleaning schedule for all residents.

### Class: `CalendarScreen` (StatelessWidget)
```dart
class CalendarScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  const CalendarScreen({super.key});
}
```

### Layout:
The screen shows a list of upcoming schedule entries. No external calendar package — build a simple custom list view.

**Header (inside Padding 16):**
- Text "Calendario de Aseo" (titleLarge, bold)
- SizedBox(height: 4)
- Text "Próximas fechas de limpieza" (bodyMedium, secondary color)

**Schedule list (ListView.builder inside Expanded):**
Get all schedules from MockData.schedules, sorted by date ascending. For each schedule entry, find the corresponding user from MockData.users.

Each entry is a Card (margin: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 16, vertical: 4)):
```dart
Card(
  child: ListTile(
    leading: CircleAvatar(
      backgroundColor: _isCurrentWeek(schedule) ? colorScheme.primary : colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest,
      foregroundColor: _isCurrentWeek(schedule) ? colorScheme.onPrimary : colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
      child: Text(user.name[0]), // First letter of name
    ),
    title: Text(user.name),
    subtitle: Text("${user.apartment} — Semana del ${_formatDate(schedule.date)}"),
    trailing: schedule.isCompleted
      ? Icon(Icons.check_circle, color: colorScheme.primary)
      : _isCurrentWeek(schedule)
        ? Chip(label: Text("Esta semana"), backgroundColor: colorScheme.primaryContainer)
        : null,
  ),
)
```

### Helper methods:
- `_isCurrentWeek(CleaningSchedule schedule)`: returns true if schedule.date falls within the current Monday-Sunday range
- `_formatDate(DateTime date)`: returns date formatted as "dd/MM/yyyy"
- `_isCurrentUser(CleaningSchedule schedule)`: returns true if schedule.userId == MockData.currentUser.id

### Visual distinction for current user entries:
If the schedule belongs to the current user, add a subtle left border or use `Card` with a `shape` that has a left-side colored border (4px wide, colorScheme.primary). This helps the user quickly spot their own entries.

### Month separator:
When the month changes between entries, insert a Padding with a Text showing the month name in Spanish (e.g., "Marzo 2026", "Abril 2026") styled as titleSmall with secondary color. Use a simple map for Spanish month names:
```dart
static const List<String> _monthNames = [
  "Enero", "Febrero", "Marzo", "Abril", "Mayo", "Junio",
  "Julio", "Agosto", "Septiembre", "Octubre", "Noviembre", "Diciembre",
];
```

## Do not
- Use any external calendar packages (no table_calendar, syncfusion, etc.)
- Add date picker or interactive calendar grid
- Make any async calls
- Import Supabase
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart exists with CalendarScreen StatelessWidget
2. Shows header "Calendario de Aseo" with subtitle "Próximas fechas de limpieza"
3. Lists all 8 schedule entries from MockData sorted by date
4. Each entry shows user name, apartment, and week start date formatted as dd/MM/yyyy
5. Completed schedules show a check_circle icon
6. Current week entry shows a "Esta semana" chip
7. Current user entries have a visual distinction (colored left border on card)
8. Month separators appear when month changes between entries (Spanish month names)
9. CircleAvatar shows first letter of user name with primary color for current week
10. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
11. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed

title: Integrate screens into app shell — replace placeholders with real screen widgets
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do
Update the CleanApp widget in lib/app.dart to replace the placeholder widgets with the real screen widgets.

### Changes to _CleanAppState:

1. Replace the `_screens` placeholder list. The screens list must be rebuilt on each build() call because HomeScreen needs a callback that calls setState:

```dart
List<Widget> get _screens => [
  HomeScreen(onNavigateToActivities: () => setState(() => _currentIndex = 1)),
  const ActivitiesScreen(),
  const CalendarScreen(),
];
```

2. Add these imports at the top of lib/app.dart:
```dart
import "package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/home_screen.dart";
import "package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/activities_screen.dart";
import "package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/calendar_screen.dart";
```

3. Keep everything else unchanged — the AppBar, NavigationBar, theme toggle all stay the same.

### Important: IndexedStack
Consider wrapping the body in an `IndexedStack` instead of just `_screens[_currentIndex]` to preserve state across tab switches (so Activities checkboxes are not reset when switching tabs):
```dart
body: IndexedStack(
  index: _currentIndex,
  children: _screens,
),
```

## Do not
- Change the NavigationBar destinations or labels
- Change the theme toggle behavior
- Modify any screen files — only modify lib/app.dart
- Add new dependencies or packages
- Remove the AppBar or any existing functionality

title: Add Comment model class
description:
## Files
- lib/models/comment.dart (create)

## What to do
Create an immutable data model for anonymous comments.

### Class: `Comment`
```dart
class Comment {
  final String id;
  final String scheduleId;   // which cleaning schedule this comment is about
  final String message;
  final DateTime createdAt;

  const Comment({
    required this.id,
    required this.scheduleId,
    required this.message,
    required this.createdAt,
  });
}
```

Key design decisions:
- No `senderId` field — comments are anonymous by design. The sender is never stored.
- `scheduleId` links the comment to a specific cleaning schedule (and thus to the responsible user for that week).
- No `recipientId` — the recipient is implicitly the user assigned to the schedule.

## Do not
- Add JSON serialization (future backend work)
- Add any flutter imports (only dart:core needed)
- Add a `senderId` or any field that could identify the sender
- Add business logic or validation

title: Epic: Limpy v2 — Renaming, UX fixes, real-time updates, notifications, and UI redesign
description:
## Goals
This epic covers all v2 improvements for the Limpy app:
1. Branding: Replace all CleanApp/flutter_cleanapp display names with Limpy
2. Admin panel fixes: Fix task ordering (ascending), add description field to tasks
3. Cleaning period: Change from weekly to 3-day periods with auto-date generation and countdown notifications
4. Real-time updates: App data should refresh without closing/reopening using Supabase Realtime
5. Prorroga calendar sync: When a prorroga is accepted, calendar dates must update and show swap icon
6. Notifications: Local notifications for comments, cleaning period countdown, and important events
7. UI/UX redesign: 3-dot menu in AppBar (theme, logout, profile), profile management screen, floating bottom nav bar

## Context
- Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime)
- State management: StatefulWidget + setState (no Provider/Bloc)
- The tasks table needs a description_task TEXT column added in the DB
- No notification system exists yet — needs flutter_local_notifications package
- No real-time subscriptions exist — only manual fetch-on-load pattern

## Success Criteria
- All user-facing strings say Limpy, zero instances of CleanApp as display text
- Tasks appear 1,2,3... (ascending) in admin panel with editable descriptions
- Admin picks 1 date and 3 consecutive days auto-created; countdown notifications sent
- Data refreshes in real-time across all screens without app restart
- Accepted prorrogas update calendar entries with swap icon
- Local notifications for comments, cleaning reminders, and prorrogas
- AppBar has 3-dot menu; profile edit screen exists; bottom nav is floating with rounded corners

title: Implement 3-day cleaning period with auto-date generation
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify)
- lib/models/cleaning_schedule.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

Change the cleaning period system from weekly (1 date per schedule) to a 3-day period. When admin selects a date, 3 consecutive schedule entries are auto-created. The period is configurable but defaults to 3 days.

### 1. Schedule Management — lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart:
In `_showAddDialog()`:
- After admin picks a user and a date, auto-create 3 consecutive schedule entries (the selected date + 2 more days)
- Replace the single `createSchedule()` call with a loop that creates 3 schedules:
  ```dart
  for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    final date = selectedDate.add(Duration(days: i));
    await SupabaseService.instance.createSchedule(
      CleaningSchedule(id: '', userId: selectedUser!.id, date: date),
    );
  }
  ```
- Add a label in the dialog: 'Se crearán 3 días consecutivos de aseo a partir de la fecha seleccionada'

### 2. Home Screen — lib/screens/home_screen.dart:
Change the current week detection logic to work with 3-day periods instead of weekly:
- Instead of finding the schedule for the current Monday-Sunday week, find any schedule entry where the date matches today or is within the user's active cleaning period
- The user is 'responsible' if there exists a schedule entry with their userId where the date is today or within the last 2 days (i.e., the 3-day window starting from the first date)
- Group consecutive schedule entries for the same user to determine the cleaning period

Simpler approach: find all schedules for the current user where date >= today - 2 days AND date <= today. If any exist and are not completed, the user is responsible.

Update the date display: instead of 'Semana del DD/MM al DD/MM', show 'Periodo de aseo: DD/MM - DD/MM' (first date to last date of the 3-day period).

### 3. Calendar Screen — lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart:
- Update the grouping logic: instead of grouping by week, group consecutive dates for the same user as a single 'period'
- Show 'Periodo del DD/MM al DD/MM' instead of 'Semana del DD/MM/YYYY'
- The 'current period' highlight should apply to any schedule entry where the date falls within today's active period

### 4. Service — lib/data/supabase_service.dart:
- Update `getCurrentWeekSchedule()` to `getCurrentPeriodSchedules()`:
  ```dart
  Future<List<CleaningSchedule>> getCurrentPeriodSchedules() async {
    final now = DateTime.now();
    final today = DateTime(now.year, now.month, now.day);
    final threeDaysAgo = today.subtract(const Duration(days: 2));
    final tomorrow = today.add(const Duration(days: 1));
    final data = await SupabaseConfig.client
        .from('schedules')
        .select()
        .gte('date', threeDaysAgo.toIso8601String().split('T').first)
        .lt('date', tomorrow.toIso8601String().split('T').first)
        .order('date');
    return data.map((json) => CleaningSchedule.fromJson(json)).toList();
  }
  ```

- Do NOT remove the weekly grouping entirely — just adapt it to 3-day periods
- Do NOT hardcode the period length everywhere — use a constant: `static const int cleaningPeriodDays = 3;` in SupabaseService or a config

title: Epic: Cycle-based schedule generation & calendar grid view
description:
## Goals
Add two major features to the calendar/schedule system:

1. **Cycle-based schedule generation (admin)** — When admin manages the calendar, provide a new option to generate schedules by cycles. Parameters:
   - Users: multi-select which users participate in the cycle
   - Start date: when the first period begins
   - Cleaning period per user: number of days (default 3)
   - Number of cycles: how many times to repeat the full rotation

   Each user gets a cleaning period of N days, starting 7 days after the previous user's start date. After all users complete one cycle, the next cycle repeats the same order.

   Example (3 users, 3-day period, start March 7):
   - Cycle 1: User1 Mar 7-9, User2 Mar 14-16, User3 Mar 21-23
   - Cycle 2: User1 Mar 28-30, User2 Apr 4-6, User3 Apr 11-13

2. **Calendar grid view** — Add a toggle in the calendar screen to switch between the current list view and a calendar grid view (month grid showing days with colored indicators for who has cleaning duty).

## Context
- Current schedule management screen (schedule_management_screen.dart) has a simple 'add single date' dialog that creates 3 consecutive days for one user
- Calendar screen (calendar_screen.dart) currently only has a list view with period grouping
- The 7-day gap between users means each user starts on the same weekday
- Schedules are stored as individual date rows in the DB (one row per day)
- SupabaseService.cleaningPeriodDays is currently hardcoded to 3

## Success Criteria
- Admin can generate schedules by cycles with all 4 parameters
- Preview of generated dates shown before confirming
- Calendar screen has a toggle between list and grid views
- Grid view shows a month calendar with colored day indicators
- All existing functionality preserved (edit, delete, prórrogas, realtime)

Add a new 'Generar por ciclos' option to the schedule management screen. This opens a full-screen dialog/page where the admin configures cycle parameters, previews the generated dates, and confirms to save them all to the database.

### Step 1: Add `createSchedulesBatch` to SupabaseService

```dart
/// Inserts multiple cleaning schedules in a single batch operation.
Future<void> createSchedulesBatch(List<CleaningSchedule> schedules) async {
  await SupabaseConfig.client
      .from('schedules')
      .insert(schedules.map((s) => s.toJson()).toList());
}
```

### Step 2: Add 'Generar por ciclos' button to schedule management screen

In `schedule_management_screen.dart`, change the single FAB to a `Column` with two FABs (or use a `FloatingActionButton.extended` + a second small FAB). Better approach: replace the single FAB with a `SpeedDial`-style approach using two FABs stacked vertically:

```dart
appBar: AppBar(

),

This should navigate to a new full-screen dialog (use `Navigator.push` with a new `StatefulWidget` class defined in the same file, or use `showModalBottomSheet` with `isScrollControlled: true`).

### Step 4: Build `_CycleGeneratorPage` widget

A `StatefulWidget` with the following UI:

**AppBar**: 'Generar por Ciclos' with back button

**Body** (scrollable Column):

1. **User selection** — A section titled 'Usuarios' with a list of `CheckboxListTile` widgets, one per user from the `_users` list. Each shows `user.name — user.room`. Admin can check/uncheck users. At least 1 must be selected. Add a 'Seleccionar todos' / 'Deseleccionar todos' toggle button.

   **Simplest approach**: Use `CheckboxListTile` in a regular `ListView`. The rotation order follows the order users appear in the list (which is alphabetical from `getUsers()`). Don't add reordering — keep it simple.

2. **Start date** — A row with 'Fecha inicial: DD/MM/YYYY' and a 'Seleccionar' button that opens `showDatePicker`. Default: today.

title: Epic: Fix auth flow, rename Departamento→Cuarto, add prórroga (extension request) system
description:
## Goals
1. **Fix auth bug**: After login/register, app stays on auth screen. Root cause: race condition in profile loading + missing error recovery in _loadCurrentUser(). The onAuthStateChange listener calls _loadCurrentUser() which queries profiles table with .single() — if the profile row doesn't exist yet (signup trigger delay) or the query fails, _isAuthenticated stays false and user is stuck.
2. **Rename Departamento → Cuarto**: All UI labels, hints, model field names, DB column, and variable names should use 'room' (cuarto) instead of 'apartment' (departamento).
3. **Prórroga (extension request) system**: Users can request an extension for their assigned cleaning week. This notifies the admin and the next person in the schedule. If the next person accepts, they swap — the requester moves to the next week and the acceptor takes the current week.

## Key Constraints
- No external state management (setState only)
- All UI text in Spanish
- Supabase backend with RLS
- Must pass: dart format, flutter analyze, flutter test
- Architecture: SupabaseService singleton, models with fromJson/toJson

## Scope
~12 tasks across auth fix, rename, DB migration, prórroga model+service+UI+notifications
close_reason: Closed

description:
## Files

## What to do

### In `supabase_service.dart` — `getCurrentUser()`:
- Replace `.single()` with `.maybeSingle()` so it returns `null` instead of throwing when no profile row exists yet
- Add a retry mechanism: if `maybeSingle()` returns null AND `auth.currentUser` is not null, wait 1 second and retry up to 3 times (the signup trigger may not have completed yet)
- Signature stays: `Future<UserModel?> getCurrentUser() async`

### In `app.dart` — `_loadCurrentUser()`:
- Wrap the `SupabaseService.instance.getCurrentUser()` call in a try/catch

- Add a retry button or auto-retry after 2 seconds

### In `app.dart` — `_checkAuth()`:
- Currently only checks `currentSession != null`. Also verify the session is not expired before calling `_loadCurrentUser()`

### In `app.dart` — `onAuthStateChange` listener:
- For `AuthChangeEvent.signedIn`, call `_loadCurrentUser()` (already does this — keep it)
- For `AuthChangeEvent.userUpdated`, also call `_loadCurrentUser()` to refresh profile data

### In `app.dart` — build method:
- When `_isAuthenticated == false` and `_isLoadingUser == false` and there IS a current auth session (edge case: profile load failed but user is authenticated), show a retry screen instead of AuthScreen. The retry screen should have a "Reintentar" button that calls `_loadCurrentUser()` and a "Cerrar sesión" button that calls `_logout()`.

- Do NOT add any external packages
- Do NOT change the onAuthStateChange event types beyond what is specified

title: Show prórroga status indicator on CalendarScreen schedule entries
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

Enhance the CalendarScreen to show when a schedule entry has a pending or accepted extension request.

### New imports:
- `import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/extension_request.dart';`

### New state:
- `List<ExtensionRequest> _extensionRequests = []`

### Changes to `_loadSchedules()`:
After loading schedules and users, also load extension requests:
```dart
final requests = await SupabaseService.instance.getExtensionRequestsForUser(widget.currentUser.id);
```
Wait — this only gets requests for the current user. For the calendar, we want to show indicators on ANY schedule that has a pending request. But non-admin users can only see their own requests due to RLS.

**Approach**: 
- For admin users: call `getAllExtensionRequests()` (from task .8)
- For non-admin users: call `getExtensionRequestsForUser(widget.currentUser.id)`
- Store in `_extensionRequests`

### Helper method:
```dart
ExtensionRequest? _getRequestForSchedule(String scheduleId) {
  try {
    return _extensionRequests.firstWhere((r) => r.scheduleId == scheduleId);
  } catch (_) {
    return null;
  }
}
```

### UI changes in the ListView itemBuilder:
For each schedule entry, check `_getRequestForSchedule(schedule.id)`:
- If a **pending** request exists: add a small `Chip` below the existing subtitle text saying "⏳ Prórroga pendiente" with `colorScheme.tertiaryContainer` background
- If an **accepted** request exists: add a small `Chip` saying "🔄 Prórroga aceptada" with `colorScheme.secondaryContainer` background
- If **rejected**: no indicator needed

Implementation: wrap the existing `subtitle` in a Column and add the Chip conditionally:
```dart
subtitle: Column(
  crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
  children: [
    Text('${user.room} — Semana del ${_formatDate(schedule.date)}'),
    if (request != null && request.isPending)
      Chip(
        label: const Text('Prórroga pendiente'),
        avatar: const Icon(Icons.schedule, size: 16),
        visualDensity: VisualDensity.compact,
        backgroundColor: colorScheme.tertiaryContainer,
      ),
    if (request != null && request.status == ExtensionRequestStatus.accepted)
      Chip(
        label: const Text('Prórroga aceptada'),
        avatar: const Icon(Icons.swap_horiz, size: 16),
        visualDensity: VisualDensity.compact,
        backgroundColor: colorScheme.secondaryContainer,
      ),
  ],
),
```

## Dont
- Do NOT modify the FAB or admin navigation
- Do NOT modify the month header logic
- Do NOT add any new screens or dialogs

1. Calendar entries with pending extension requests show a "Prórroga pendiente" chip

4. Admin users see indicators on all schedules; regular users see only their own

title: Add SQL migration for extension_requests table and RLS policies
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/003_create_extension_requests.sql (create)

## What to do

Create a new SQL migration file that creates the `extension_requests` table for the prórroga system.

### Table schema:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.extension_requests (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.schedules(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  requester_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  next_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'accepted', 'rejected')),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);
```

### RLS policies:
1. Enable RLS on the table
2. **SELECT**: Authenticated users can see extension_requests where they are the requester_id, next_user_id, OR they are an admin
3. **INSERT**: Authenticated users can insert rows where requester_id = auth.uid()
4. **UPDATE**: Authenticated users can update rows where next_user_id = auth.uid() (the person accepting/rejecting) OR they are an admin
5. **DELETE**: Only admins can delete

### Index:
- Add index on `schedule_id` for fast lookups
- Add index on `status` for filtering pending requests

## Dont
- Do NOT modify existing tables
- Do NOT modify existing migration files (001, 002)
- Do NOT add any Dart code in this task
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at supabase/migrations/003_create_extension_requests.sql
2. Table has all 7 columns: id, schedule_id, requester_id, next_user_id, status, created_at, resolved_at
3. RLS is enabled with 4 policies (select, insert, update, delete)
4. Status CHECK constraint includes pending, accepted, rejected
5. Foreign keys reference schedules and profiles with ON DELETE CASCADE
6. Two indexes exist (schedule_id, status)
7. SQL is syntactically valid (no typos in keywords)

title: Add extension request CRUD methods to SupabaseService
description:
## Files
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify)

## What to do

Add 6 new methods to SupabaseService under a new `// --- Extension Requests ---` section at the bottom of the class.

### Import:
Add `import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/extension_request.dart';` at the top.

### Methods:

1. **`Future<List<ExtensionRequest>> getExtensionRequestsForUser(String userId)`**
   - Query `extension_requests` table where `requester_id == userId` OR `next_user_id == userId`
   - Order by `created_at` descending
   - Return mapped list of ExtensionRequest.fromJson

2. **`Future<List<ExtensionRequest>> getPendingExtensionRequests()`**
   - Query `extension_requests` table where `status == 'pending'`
   - Order by `created_at` descending
   - Return mapped list

3. **`Future<ExtensionRequest?> getPendingRequestForSchedule(String scheduleId)`**
   - Query `extension_requests` where `schedule_id == scheduleId` AND `status == 'pending'`
   - Use `.maybeSingle()`
   - Return ExtensionRequest.fromJson or null

4. **`Future<void> createExtensionRequest({required String scheduleId, required String requesterId, required String nextUserId})`**
   - Insert into `extension_requests` with the 3 required fields
   - Status defaults to 'pending' in DB

5. **`Future<void> acceptExtensionRequest(String requestId)`**
   - Update the extension_request row: set `status = 'accepted'`, `resolved_at = now()` (use `DateTime.now().toIso8601String()`)
   - Then perform the schedule swap:
     a. Fetch the extension request to get scheduleId, requesterId, nextUserId
     b. Fetch all schedules ordered by date
     c. Find the current schedule (scheduleId) — this is the requester's week
     d. Find the next schedule where userId == nextUserId and date is AFTER the current schedule's date
     e. Swap the user_ids: update current schedule's userId to nextUserId, update next schedule's userId to requesterId
   - This is the core swap logic. If the next schedule doesnt exist, just update the current schedule's userId to nextUserId (the requester loses their turn).

6. **`Future<void> rejectExtensionRequest(String requestId)`**
   - Update the extension_request row: set `status = 'rejected'`, `resolved_at = now()`

## Dont
- Do NOT modify any existing methods
- Do NOT add UI code
- Do NOT add any new packages
- Do NOT change the SupabaseService singleton pattern
acceptance_criteria:
1. SupabaseService has 6 new methods under "Extension Requests" section
2. getExtensionRequestsForUser filters by requester_id OR next_user_id using .or() filter
3. getPendingExtensionRequests filters by status=pending
4. getPendingRequestForSchedule uses .maybeSingle() and returns nullable
5. createExtensionRequest inserts with 3 required fields
6. acceptExtensionRequest updates status AND performs the schedule user_id swap
7. rejectExtensionRequest updates status and resolved_at only
8. Import for extension_request.dart is present
9. flutter analyze reports no issues
10. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes

title: Create Supabase database tables via SQL migrations
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql (create)

## What to do
Create a SQL migration file that documents the exact SQL to run in the Supabase SQL editor. The worker must also RUN this SQL against the Supabase project.

### Connect to Supabase:
Use the Supabase Management API or the `supabase` CLI if available. If neither is available, create the SQL file and document that it must be run manually in the Supabase dashboard SQL editor.

### SQL to create:

```sql
-- 1. Profiles table (extends auth.users)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.profiles (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  apartment TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user' CHECK (role IN ('user', 'admin')),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE public.profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Policies: users can read all profiles, update only their own
CREATE POLICY "Profiles are viewable by authenticated users"
  ON public.profiles FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile"
  ON public.profiles FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (auth.uid() = id);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage all profiles"
  ON public.profiles FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin')
  );

-- Auto-create profile on signup
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, name, apartment, role)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>name, ),
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>apartment, ),
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>role, user)
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user();

-- 2. Schedules table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.schedules (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  date DATE NOT NULL,
  is_completed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

ALTER TABLE public.schedules ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Schedules are viewable by authenticated users"
  ON public.schedules FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage schedules"
  ON public.schedules FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin')
  );

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own schedule completion"
  ON public.schedules FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (user_id = auth.uid())
  WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid());

-- 3. Tasks table (defines the checklist items)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.tasks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  sort_order INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

ALTER TABLE public.tasks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Tasks are viewable by authenticated users"
  ON public.tasks FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage tasks"
  ON public.tasks FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin')
  );

-- Insert default tasks
INSERT INTO public.tasks (title, sort_order) VALUES
  (Lavar

4. schedules table has columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (FK to profiles), date, is_completed, created_at

### Reorder:
On ReorderableListView.onReorder, update sort_order for affected tasks:
```dart
void _onReorder(int oldIndex, int newIndex) async {
  if (newIndex > oldIndex) newIndex--;
  final task = _tasks.removeAt(oldIndex);
  _tasks.insert(newIndex, task);
  setState(() {});
  // Update sort_order for all tasks
  for (var i = 0; i < _tasks.length; i++) {
    await SupabaseService.instance.updateTask(_tasks[i].id, sortOrder: i + 1);
  }
  await _loadTasks();
}
```

### Load tasks: use SupabaseService.instance.getAllTasks() (includes inactive)

- Delete tasks permanently from this screen (only deactivate via isActive toggle)

title: Fix profile navigation from 3-dot PopupMenuButton
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

The 'Administrar perfil' option in the 3-dot PopupMenuButton does nothing when tapped. The root cause is that PopupMenuItem wraps a ListTile child, and the ListTile's InkWell absorbs the tap gesture, preventing the PopupMenuItem's onTap/value selection from firing.

### Fix:
Replace the ListTile children inside PopupMenuItem with simple Row widgets that don't have their own tap handlers. The PopupMenuItem already handles the tap — the child should be a passive display widget.

### Current code (lines 181-215 in app.dart):
The itemBuilder returns PopupMenuItem widgets with ListTile children. ListTile has its own InkWell which conflicts with PopupMenuItem's tap detection.

### Replace each PopupMenuItem child from ListTile to Row:
```dart
itemBuilder: (context) => [
  PopupMenuItem(
    value: 'theme',
    child: Row(
      children: [
        Icon(_themeModeIcon),
        const SizedBox(width: 12),
        const Text('Cambiar tema'),
      ],
    ),
  ),
  PopupMenuItem(
    value: 'profile',
    child: const Row(
      children: [
        Icon(Icons.person),
        SizedBox(width: 12),
        Text('Administrar perfil'),
      ],
    ),
  ),
  const PopupMenuDivider(),
  PopupMenuItem(
    value: 'logout',
    child: Row(
      children: [
        Icon(Icons.logout, color: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.error),
        const SizedBox(width: 12),
        Text(
          'Cerrar sesion',
          style: TextStyle(color: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.error),
        ),
      ],
    ),
  ),
],
```

### Also fix the profile navigation to use async/await properly:
The current code uses .then() which may lose context. Change to:
```dart
case 'profile':
  final changed = await Navigator.push<bool>(
    context,
    MaterialPageRoute(
      builder: (_) => ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
    ),
  );
  if (changed == true && mounted) _loadCurrentUser();
```

Note: the onSelected callback needs to be async for this. Change:
```dart
onSelected: (value) {
```
to:
```dart
onSelected: (value) async {
```

## Don't
- Do NOT change the menu items or their order
- Do NOT change the theme toggle or logout logic
- Do NOT modify ProfileScreen itself

title: Group calendar entries by cleaning period into single cards
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

The calendar currently renders one Card per schedule row. Since each cleaning period creates 3 consecutive schedule entries for the same user, the calendar shows 3 separate cards. Instead, group consecutive same-user schedules into a single card showing the date range.

### Current behavior (lines 168-189):
The code iterates over sorted schedules and creates one `_ScheduleEntry` per schedule. Each entry becomes a Card in the ListView.

### Required changes:

1. **Replace `_ScheduleEntry` with `_PeriodEntry`** — a new sealed class variant that holds a `List<CleaningSchedule>` (the grouped schedules) and a `UserModel`:
```dart
final class _PeriodEntry extends _ListItem {
  _PeriodEntry({required this.schedules, required this.user});
  final List<CleaningSchedule> schedules;
  final UserModel user;
}
```

2. **Group consecutive same-user schedules** — After sorting schedules by date ascending (line 168-169), iterate and group consecutive entries with the same `userId` into a single `_PeriodEntry`. Algorithm:
   - Walk the sorted list. While the next schedule has the same `userId` as the current group, add it to the group.
   - When the userId changes (or list ends), emit a `_PeriodEntry` with the accumulated group.
   - Month headers should be emitted based on the **first** date of each group.

3. **Update the Card rendering** (lines 231-296) to use `_PeriodEntry`:
   - The subtitle should show the date range: `'<room> — Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY al DD/MM/YYYY'` using the first and last schedule dates in the group. If the group has only 1 schedule, show just `'<room> — Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY'`.
   - `_isCurrentPeriod` should return true if **any** schedule in the group falls within the current period window.
   - `_isCurrentUser` should check the first schedule's userId (they're all the same).
   - `_getRequestForSchedule` should check **all** schedules in the group and return the first non-null result.
   - The `isCompleted` trailing icon should show if **all** schedules in the group are completed.
   - The `currentUser` border highlight should apply if the group belongs to the current user.

4. **Remove the old `_ScheduleEntry` class** — it's replaced by `_PeriodEntry`.

### Edge cases:
- A single schedule (not part of a 3-day period) should still render correctly as a group of 1.
- If the same user has two non-consecutive periods (e.g., dates 1-3 and 10-12), they should be two separate cards because other users' schedules appear between them.
- Month headers: if a period spans two months (e.g., Jan 31, Feb 1, Feb 2), emit the month header based on the first date only.

## Don't
- Do NOT change the data fetching logic (`_loadSchedules`)
- Do NOT change the FAB or admin navigation
- Do NOT modify SupabaseService or any model files
- Do NOT change the Realtime subscription logic
- Do NOT change the extension request (prórroga) chip display logic — just adapt it to check all schedules in the group

2. Card subtitle shows date range: 'Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY al DD/MM/YYYY' for multi-day periods
3. Card subtitle shows single date: 'Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY' for single-schedule groups
4. Current period highlighting works correctly (checks any schedule in group)
5. Current user border highlighting works correctly
6. Completed check icon shows only when ALL schedules in group are completed
7. Prórroga chips still display correctly
8. Month headers still appear correctly (based on first date of each group)
9. Single-schedule groups render correctly
10. Non-consecutive same-user periods appear as separate cards
11. flutter analyze reports no issues
12. flutter test — all tests pass
13. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes

title: Change app icon to icon_limpy.png on all platforms using flutter_launcher_icons
description:
## Files
- pubspec.yaml (modify — add flutter_launcher_icons dev dependency and config)
- icon_limpy.png (read — source icon at project root, 96x96 RGBA PNG)
- android/app/src/main/res/mipmap-*/ic_launcher.png (generated)
- ios/Runner/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/* (generated)
- macos/Runner/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/* (generated)
- web/favicon.png (generated)
- web/icons/* (generated)
- windows/runner/resources/app_icon.ico (generated)
- linux/ (generated if applicable)

## What to do

Replace the default Flutter app icon with `icon_limpy.png` on all platforms. The source image is only 96x96 pixels — use the `flutter_launcher_icons` package to handle resizing and platform-specific icon generation.

### Steps:

1. **Add `flutter_launcher_icons` as a dev dependency**:
```bash
flutter pub add --dev flutter_launcher_icons
```

2. **Add configuration to `pubspec.yaml`** at the root level (not under dependencies):
```yaml
flutter_launcher_icons:
  android: true
  ios: true
  macos: true
  web:
    generate: true
    image_path: "icon_limpy.png"
    background_color: "#ffffff"
    theme_color: "#ffffff"
  windows:
    generate: true
    image_path: "icon_limpy.png"
    icon_size: 48
  image_path: "icon_limpy.png"
  min_sdk_android: 21
  remove_alpha_ios: true
```

3. **Run the icon generator**:
```bash
dart run flutter_launcher_icons
```

4. **Verify** the generated icons exist in all platform directories.

### Important notes:
- The source icon is 96x96 which is small. The package will upscale it for larger sizes (like iOS 1024x1024). This is acceptable — the user provided this icon knowing its size.
- If `flutter_launcher_icons` fails or produces poor results due to the small source size, fall back to manual resizing with ImageMagick (`convert` command) for each required size.
- The `remove_alpha_ios: true` flag is needed because iOS doesn't allow transparency in app icons.

### Android icon sizes needed:
- mipmap-mdpi: 48x48
